summary: Central governance validates API-linked receipts and monitoring artifacts but does not own service-local runtimes or execution tooling.
central_allows: JSON contracts for API receipts and monitoring artifacts.
Schema validation for service snapshots and hygiene reports.
GitHub workflow enforcement for drift detection.
Repository-wide read scans during linking, hygiene review, and release gating.
Receipt-gated cleanup outside governance/ when the approved mutation scope explicitly allows it.
central_forbids: Treating API telemetry as a substitute for human approval or release gates.
Mutating paths outside governance/ without explicit approved scope and receipt.
service_runtime_rule: API clients stay in the service repository.
Browser automation and runtime tools stay in the service repository.
All external execution must emit receipt-linked artifacts.
Out-of-governance cleanup is valid only when the link approval explicitly permits the target scope.
Any out-of-boundary mutation must remain reviewable by evidence and rollback notes.
